title: Monkeypox Vaccination | Monkeypox | CDC
text:
e vaccine is injected in the fat layer underneath the skin on the back of your upper arm (the area above the tricep muscle). 
Intradermal, which means the vaccine is given between the top layers of your skin in the forearm, upper back below the shoulder blade, or shoulder above the deltoid muscle. 
You and your provider can discuss which method to use.
Get both recommended doses of monkeypox vaccine 
Whether you get the vaccine subcutaneously or intradermally, you should still get 2 doses. Although findings suggest that the first dose of JYNNEOS vaccine gives some protection against monkeypox, 2 doses are recommended to provide stronger protection. The second dose should be given 4 weeks (28 days) after the first dose. If you can't get your second dose on time, you should get it as soon as possible to complete the series. 
Considerations for vaccine method 
Intradermal vaccination may leave a mark that others can see on your forearm. If that concerns you, you can get it subcutaneously or in the skin of your upper back or shoulder. 
If you have ever had keloid scars (thick, raised scars), ask for the vaccine to be given to you subcutaneously. 
If you are under 18 years of age, you should be given the vaccine subcutaneously.
